ksa_text: Skill in designing and executing adversarial testing for AI systems, including red-teaming LLMs, testing model robustness to input perturbation, and documenting vulnerability findings.

dcwf.top_element_label: Ability to apply system design tools, methods, and techniques, including automated systems analysis and design tools.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Included: skill maps directly to AI Ethics Board Member scope — RSK-specific signals detected ("vulnerabilit", "adversarial test", "test.{0,15} ?:ai|model|algorithm|system"). STRM composite is strong (0.5428); note that framework scores routinely underweight risk management and governance vocabulary, so text-based signal carries primary weight here. Seniority is appropriate: the KSA describes practice-level execution, not enterprise strategy.

pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: External validation present: 1 match(es) in RSK job postings or certification scope. Competency appears in RSK role descriptions — sufficient to uphold.
